search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG185 PCI antithrombotic risk discharge follow-up rehabilitation
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic antiplatelet duration, medicine change, test, rehabilitation, referral, or activity advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The PCI report, indication, anatomy, stents, complications, and current medicines are required.
No medicine, dose, duration, investigation, or advice was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults after PCI in an acute-coronary-syndrome pathway.
UAE_applicability: NICE evidence requires UAE interventional-cardiology, formulary, rehabilitation, emergency, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG185 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: NG185 supports documenting the PCI and antithrombotic-risk context, already selected treatment, relevant adverse-effect or bleeding discussion, and clinician review without changing therapy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports PCI context and clinician-led bleeding, thromboembolic, cardiovascular, and antithrombotic-risk review without mapping medicine names or doses into generated content.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The discharge and rehabilitation section supports documenting information already provided, recovery, questions, and clinician-arranged follow-up after PCI without generating instructions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

unresolved_source_gaps: Procedure date and indication, coronary anatomy, access site, stent details, complications, antithrombotic regimen, adherence, bleeding, recurrent symptoms, rehabilitation, test results, treatment, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
